name: indexer_down_burst
description: Combines high concurrency with a downed indexer to block the m_indexerQueue sink (also UNLIMITED_QUEUE_SIZE) while m_workersQueue keeps receiving. Aggressive version of indexer_down.json (which uses 50 agents): 500 agents + 5 min allow observing OOM if both queues accumulate without bounds.
notes: external_actions are fired automatically by run_benchmark.sh and logged to results_*/external_actions.log. NOTE: at_sec=10 stops the indexer DURING the sender's ~70s setup phase, so all sessions hit the handleStart Offline guard rail (StartAck Status::Offline) before reaching the queue. Use indexer_down_midflight.json to test the queue-under-pressure scenario with the indexer down mid-session.
Dual hypothesis: with the indexer stopped, m_indexerQueue retains Response{} and m_workersQueue accumulates raw bytes while workers wait to push to the sink.
Expect: end_ack_error > 0 during the downtime window, RSS climbing in a ramp.
If RSS doesn't drop after restarting the indexer, the queue doesn't drain after the sink returns → real leak, not just transient backpressure.
rss_growth_mb_max=1500 is intentionally high: we want PASS if growth is bounded; the exact value is calibrated after the first run.
